title: ekebergia benguelensis
intro: Ekebergia benguelensis, also known as the Angola tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Meliaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ekebergia benguelensis include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Ekebergia benguelensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion of its bark, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
